request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Federal Reserve rate decision — March 2026 FOMC meeting
Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome announced by the Federal Open Market Committee at the
conclusion of its March 17-18, 2026 meeting. Settlement is the official FOMC
statement on the federal funds target range published 2026-03-18.

Standard FOMC-decision outcome buckets cover changes of -50 bps, -25 bps,
0 bps (hold), +25 bps, +50 bps relative to the prior target range.

Conditions:
  A: Official FOMC statement (2026-03-18) — Use the official FOMC statement published 2026-03-18. Compare the announced federal funds target range to the range in effect before the meeting. If the change equals this market’s bucket, resolve YES.
  B: Fed Implementation Note/press release — If no usable statement, use the Fed’s Implementation Note or FOMC press release dated 2026-03-18 that sets the target range. If the change vs the prior range matches this market’s bucket, YES.
  C: Delayed/canceled meeting fallback — If the Mar 17-18 meeting is delayed/canceled or the statement is later, use the first official FOMC/Board release for the March 2026 decision. If the change vs prior matches this market’s bucket, YES.
Settlement entities: ['Federal Open Market Committee', 'Federal Reserve System', 'Federal Reserve Board of Governors', 'Jerome Powell', 'Department of the Treasury', 'Bureau of Labor Statistics']

Record title: November 25, 2025 - Pre-Agenda
Record date: 2025-11-25
Record regulator: STATE OF NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY DIVISION OF PENSIONS AND BENEFITS
Record topic: New Jersey Bureau of Securities
Record entities: ['Alison Keating', 'DAG Garen Gazaryan', 'DAG Matthew Melton', 'DAG Nicholas Kant', 'Lauren Sandy', 'STATE OF NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY DIVISION OF PENSIONS AND BENEFITS', 'State Police Retirement System']

response.one_line_why: State-level pre-agenda about NJ pensions/securities; unrelated to the Federal Reserve or FOMC policy, so no impact.
